paragraph.heading: Agency.—
paragraph.content: The term “agency” has the meaning given the term in section 552(f) of title 5.

paragraph.heading: Core data elements.—
paragraph.chapeau: The term “core data elements” means data elements relating to financial management, administration, or management that—

subparagraph.content: are not program-specific in nature or program-specific outcome measures, as defined in section 1115(h) of this title; and
are required by agencies for all or the vast majority of recipients of Federal awards for purposes of reporting.

paragraph.heading: Director.—
paragraph.content: The term “Director” means the Director of the Office of Management and Budget.

paragraph.heading: Executive department.—
paragraph.content: The term “Executive department” has the meaning given the term in section 101 of title 5.

subparagraph.content: means the transfer of anything of value for a public purpose of support or stimulation authorized by a law of the United States, including financial assistance and Government facilities, services, and property;
includes a grant, a subgrant, a cooperative agreement, or any other transaction; and

subparagraph.chapeau: does not include a transaction or agreement—

clause.content: that provides for conventional public information services or procurement of property or services for the direct benefit or use of the Government; or

subclause.content: direct Government cash assistance to an individual;

paragraph.heading: Secretary.—
paragraph.content: The term “Secretary” means the head of the standard-setting agency.

paragraph.heading: Standard-setting agency.—
paragraph.content: The term “standard-setting agency” means the Executive department designated under section 6402(a)(1).

paragraph.heading: State.—
paragraph.content: The term “State” means each State of the United States, the District of Columbia, each commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States, and each federally recognized Indian Tribe.

note.heading: Short Title
note.p: Pub. L. 116–103, § 1(a), Dec. 30, 2019, 133 Stat. 3266, provided that: “This Act [enacting this chapter, amending sections 7502 and 7505 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and sections 6402 and 7505 of this title] may be cited as the ‘Grant Reporting Efficiency and Agreements Transparency Act of 2019’ or the ‘GREAT Act’.”

note.heading: Rule of Construction
note.p: Pub. L. 116–103, § 8, Dec. 30, 2019, 133 Stat. 3271, provided that: “Nothing in this Act [see Short Title note set out above], or the amendments made by this Act, shall be construed to require the collection of data that is not otherwise required under any Federal law, rule, or regulation.”

note.heading: Purposes
Definitions
note.p: Pub. L. 116–103, § 3, Dec. 30, 2019, 133 Stat. 3267, provided that: “In this Act [see Short Title note set out above], the terms ‘agency’, ‘Director’, ‘Federal award’, and ‘Secretary’ have the meanings given those terms in section 6401 of title 31, United States Code, as added by section 4(a) of this Act.”
